title: sync-resolve-divergence-tests: cover the op-space resolver
body:
# Goal

Cover `Storage::resolve_divergence` (and its supporting
projection in `crates/jjf-storage/src/merge_ops.rs`) with
storage-side unit tests. The per-field merge policy from
spec ┬¦6 has no direct test coverage today ŌĆö `merge_ops.rs`'s
existing `#[cfg(test)]` block only covers `OpKey` ordering
and `truncate_to_seconds`.

End state: every branch of the per-field policy is exercised
by a focused unit test, and a regression in the resolver
fails CI immediately.

# Why now

Op-space resolution is the load-bearing primitive behind
the whole sync surface (`docs/storage-format.md` ┬¦6, epic
body of `54caedd`). Today its only coverage is integration
tests that go through the full push/pull/merge loop ŌĆö slow,
high-blast-radius, and they don't pin individual policy
decisions. Direct unit tests on `resolve_divergence` make
future changes (ticket `sync-reenable-merge-cross-check`)
safe.

This ticket BLOCKS the re-enable ticket: the new tests are
the safety net we want in place before flipping that flag.

# Scope

## Tests to add (in `crates/jjf-storage/src/merge_ops.rs`'s
`#[cfg(test)]` block)

For each scalar field (`status`, `title`, `assignee`,
`body`):

- LWW by `Jjf-At` timestamp: later op wins.
- Same `Jjf-At`: tie-break by commit hash.
- Same `(Jjf-At, commit)`: tie-break by `trailer_index`.
- Unstamped op (no `Jjf-At`) at same second as a stamped
  op: stamped wins (matches `op_key_stamped_beats_unstamped_at_same_second`'s
  invariant, applied through the resolver).

For sets (`labels`, `dependencies`):

- One side adds X, other side leaves alone ŌåÆ X present.
- One side adds X, other side removes X (after the add) ŌåÆ
  whichever comes last by op key wins.
- Both sides add X independently ŌåÆ X present (idempotent).
- One side adds X then removes; other side adds Y ŌåÆ final
  state is {Y}.

For comments:

- Both sides append distinct comments ŌåÆ union by
  `comment_id`, ordered by `created_at`.
- Both sides append a comment with identical `comment_id`
  (the storage layer's "shouldn't happen but" path) ŌåÆ one
  copy retained, the resolver does not panic.

Cross-version (v1 trailers from pre-migration commits mixed
with v2 trailers) coverage:

- A v1-trailer op and a v2-trailer op on the same field
  resolve correctly under the unified key ordering.

## Test fixtures

Inline `Op` and `HistoryEntry` constructors local to the
test module ŌĆö no jj-shell-out, no test-repo scaffolding.
Each test should be < 30 lines and read top-to-bottom.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p jjf-storage merge_ops::` runs
  the new tests; all pass.
- [ ] Every branch in `resolve_divergence` and the per-field
  reducers is hit by at least one test (eyeball check; no
  coverage tool required).
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` stays green.
- [ ] No production-code changes; this is tests-only.

# Out of scope

- Changing the merge policy.
- Touching the file-bytes merge driver (`e2e473b`-era code).
- The `touched_by_merge` cross-check (separate ticket
  `sync-reenable-merge-cross-check`).
- The dual-file path filter question (separate ticket
  `sync-investigate-dual-file-filter`).

# Pointers

- Implementation: `crates/jjf-storage/src/merge_ops.rs`
  (548 lines as of `594e257`).
- Existing tests in same file: `truncate_drops_nanos`,
  `truncate_no_nanos_is_identity`,
  `op_key_stamped_beats_unstamped_at_same_second`. Reuse
  the test-module imports.
- Spec: `docs/storage-format.md` ┬¦6 (per-field merge policy).
- Op model: `crates/jjf-storage/src/op.rs` (all `Op::*`
  variants the resolver consumes).
